sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see:
- id=13: G (dark gray), 20 pixels - this is the converted cross
- id=15, 17, 23, 43, 54: N (light green), 12 pixels each - 4 remaining green crosses
- id=20, 27, 29, 33: g (gray), 2 pixels each - small gray elements
So clicking at (19, 19) converted one green cross to dark gray. Now I need to click the remaining 4 green crosses. Let me find their positions and there are 4 remaining green crosses. Let me find their positions and click them all.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
targets = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        row = (b[0][0] + b[2][0]) // 2
        col = (b[0][1] + b[2][1]) // 2
        targets.append((row, col))
print("Remaining green crosses:", targets)
moves = []
for r, c in targets:
    moves.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c})
print("Clicking:", moves)
action(moves)
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
